text: ist 6 interesting spots in Copenhagen as the potential destinations, as well as the reason for choosing them, the admission prices, and the opening days, and list 6 important “travel tips” about Copenhagen.

Amarienborg: This is the home of the Danish royal family. It's a must-visit for its historical significance and the change of guard at 12:00. You can reach it within 10 minutes from the Copenhagen airport. The admission price is $1.25 and it opens all week.

Frederiks Kirke: Known for its modern architecture, it's a beautiful place to visit. The admission price is $20 and it opens from Tuesday to Friday.

The Little Mermaid Statue: This statue, inspired by the character from Grimm's Fairy Tales, is one of the most iconic landmarks in Copenhagen. It's free to visit and opens all week.

Noma: If you're a foodie, this world-famous restaurant is a must-visit. It offers a culinary masterpiece with a price of $100. It opens all week.

Rundetaarn: This is a 17th-century astronomy tower, offering a stunning view of the city. The admission price is DKK 40 and it opens all week.

Christiansborg Palace: One of the most significant landmarks in Copenhagen, both historically and politically. It's free to visit and opens from Tuesday to Sunday.

The type of power sockets used in Denmark are Type A and Type K. So, you might need to bring an adapter.

The official currency in Denmark is the Danish Krone (DKK). Most places do not accept euros.
